meta.title: Footer
meta.description: Site footer with logo, contact information, navigation links, social media icons, and copyright credits on a dark charcoal card layout with gradient background

blockHierarchy.description: Deep inner block hierarchy following footer block-type pattern. The footer is divided into two visual cards: a top info card (logo + contact) and a bottom card (nav + credits). The bottom card uses an outer wrapper with two inner sections.
tree: FooterNav (nav links section inside bottom card)
FooterNavColumn (nav column with heading + links) [repeatable, 6 default]
notes: FooterNav and FooterCredits share the same visual bottom card container. The outer rounded-[32px] wrapper with charcoal background is applied by Footer parent. FooterNav and FooterCredits are placed inside this wrapper, each with their own rounded-[25px] and charcoal background.

The decorative abstract shapes (2 in the wrapper area, 1 in the nav area) use mix-blend-mode darken/hue and are purely cosmetic. These should be hardcoded in the Footer parent and FooterNav PHP/TSX, not editable. They can be implemented later as a visual polish step.
The logo in FooterInfo is an editable media upload (icon + wordmark), stored as a single media ID.

footerTsx: { useBlockProps, useInnerBlocksProps, store as blockEditorStore } from '@wordpress/block-editor'
footerInfoSocialItemTsx: { ToolbarGroup, ToolbarButton, Popover, PanelBody, BaseControl, Button, ToggleControl, TextControl } from '@wordpress/components'

requiredPatterns.usage: Footer parent enforces section order with useEffect + replaceInnerBlocks
All container blocks use for child block rendering
FooterInfoSocial, FooterNav (for columns), FooterNavColumn (for items), FooterCredits (for items)
FooterNavColumnItem and FooterCreditsItem for editable links
FooterInfo (address, hours, contact text), FooterNavColumn (heading), FooterCredits (copyright, attribution)
FooterInfoSocialItem for inline SVG social icons
FooterInfoSocialItem uses toolbar link popover (not inline text)
FooterInfoSocialItem for icon upload/replace, FooterInfo for logo upload
PHP for social media icon SVGs

qualityChecklist: Footer parent has multiple: false to prevent duplicates
Footer parent uses TEMPLATE + REQUIRED + useEffect ordering pattern
Footer parent has no Appender (sections are fixed)
All section blocks have parent: ['takt/footer'] restriction
All section blocks have multiple: false
Social media items default opensInNewTab to true
Social media items use sr-only for accessibility
Social media items use theme_output_svg_or_img() in PHP
Legal links render as underlined text
TSX and PHP produce identical HTML structure
No hardcoded colors - uses theme tokens
Responsive: stacked on mobile, horizontal on desktop

footerInfo.name: logoId
footerInfo.description: Media library ID for the footer logo (icon + wordmark combined image or SVG)

footerInfo.name: addressContent
footerInfo.description: Address content with multiple campuses. HTML content with bold campus names and address text. Use <strong> for campus names.

footerInfo.name: contactContent
footerInfo.description: Contact information (phone, email). HTML content with line breaks.

footerInfo.name: socialLabel
footerInfo.description: Label above the social media icons

footerInfoSocialItem.name: link
footerInfoSocialItem.description: Social media link with URL and accessibility label. opensInNewTab defaults to true for social links.
footerInfoSocialItem.editorComponent: Custom LinkControl popover in BlockControls toolbar

footerInfoSocialItem.name: logoId
footerInfoSocialItem.description: Media library ID for the social media icon (SVG preferred for CSS fill control)
footerInfoSocialItem.editorComponent: MediaUpload in toolbar + MediaReplaceFlow + InspectorControls panel

footerNavColumn.name: heading
footerNavColumn.description: Column heading (e.g. 'About', 'Health + Dental'). Uses Anton font at 16px/1.1.

footerNavColumn.name: subHeading
footerNavColumn.description: Optional sub-heading below heading (e.g. 'EVENTS' in the Social + Community Spaces column). Uses Roboto 14px/1.1 Medium uppercase.

footerNavColumnItem.name: link
footerNavColumnItem.description: Navigation link with URL, title, and optional accessibility label

footerCredits.name: copyrightText
footerCredits.description: Copyright text, e.g. 'IGNITE. All rights reserved.' The year is auto-prepended with the copyright symbol.

footerCredits.name: attributionText
footerCredits.description: Attribution text with link. 'Takt' should be an underlined link.

footerCreditsItem.name: link
footerCreditsItem.description: Legal/policy link (e.g. Privacy Policy, Terms of Service)

footer.notes: The footer wrapper has the gradient background applied via inline style or custom CSS. It uses overflow-hidden to clip the decorative shapes. Padding is 32px on desktop, can reduce on mobile.

background.description: Linear gradient from transparent to blue-50% opacity over off-white base
background.cssValue: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(71, 203, 242, 0) 6.73%, rgba(71, 203, 242, 0.5) 100%), linear-gradient(90deg, var(--color-off-white) 0%, var(--color-off-white) 100%)
background.implementation: Apply via inline style or a custom utility class in CSS. Recommend a custom class .footer-bg in the block's scope or use Tailwind arbitrary: bg-[linear-gradient(...)]

innerWrapper.classes: flex flex-col gap-4 items-stretch relative z-1
innerWrapper.notes: z-1 to sit above decorative shapes

decorativeShapes.description: 2-3 abstract rotated shapes with mix-blend-mode. These are purely decorative, not editable.
decorativeShapes.implementation: Use absolute positioned divs with background images or inline SVGs. Apply mix-blend-darken and mix-blend-hue. Rotate with rotate-[126.04deg] and rotate-[85.89deg]. These can be implemented as pseudo-elements or hardcoded divs.
decorativeShapes.priority: LOW - implement as visual polish after core functionality works. Can be deferred.

footerInfo.notes: Top card with logo and contact info. rounded-3xl is 24px vs Figma 25px (acceptable 1px difference).

logo.notes: Logo icon + wordmark, approximately 67x58px combined. The logo is a single uploaded image/SVG.

logoImage.notes: Constrain height, auto width to maintain aspect ratio

infoRow.notes: Flex row on desktop, stacked on mobile/tablet

label.notes: Anton 16px/1.1 - section labels like Address, Hours, Contact, Follow Us

addressColumns.notes: Two campus address columns side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile

campusName.notes: Roboto 12px/1.5 Bold for campus names like 'Humber North Campus'

rightInfoGroup.classes: flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-8 lg:w-[640px] lg:shrink-0
rightInfoGroup.notes: Hours, Contact, Follow Us columns. 640px fixed width on desktop per Figma.

innerList.notes: Horizontal row of social icons with 12px gap

link.classes: block transition-colors w-6 h-6 no-underline! *:w-6 *:h-6 *:object-contain *:object-center text-white hover:text-blue
link.notes: 24x24px icons, white fill, hover to blue. Uses theme_output_svg_or_img() for SVG inline rendering.

bottomCardWrapper.description: The bottom card is a wrapper div rendered by the Footer parent that contains FooterNav and FooterCredits. It provides the outer rounded container.
bottomCardWrapper.notes: The outer wrapper has 32px border radius. FooterNav and FooterCredits sit inside.

footerNav.notes: Navigation section with 6 equal columns of links. rounded-3xl (24px) vs Figma 25px (acceptable).

innerGrid.notes: 6 equal columns on desktop, 3 on tablet, 2 on mobile. Using grid for equal column distribution.

subHeading.notes: Roboto 14px/1.1 Medium uppercase. Only shown if subHeading attribute has content. Used for 'EVENTS' sub-label.

linksList.notes: Vertical list of links with 8px gap

link.classes: text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans no-underline! hover:underline! text-white
link.notes: Roboto 12px/1.5 Medium, white text, no underline by default, underline on hover

footerCredits.notes: Credits bar with 32px horizontal padding, 16px vertical padding

innerRow.classes: flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-center gap-4 sm:gap-0
innerRow.notes: Three sections: copyright (left), attribution (center), legal links (right). Stacked on mobile.

copyright.classes: flex-1 text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans text-center sm:text-left

attribution.classes: flex-1 text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans text-center

innerList.classes: flex flex-wrap gap-6 justify-center sm:justify-end list-none
innerList.notes: Legal links with 24px gap, right-aligned on desktop

link.classes: text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans underline text-white
link.notes: Roboto 12px/1.5 Medium, white text, always underlined

behavior.footerInfo: p-4 or p-6. Info row stacks vertically. Address columns stack vertically.
behavior.footerNav: Grid cols 2. Columns stack in 2-column grid.
behavior.footerCredits: Credits sections stack vertically, centered text
behavior.logo: Stays same size or slightly smaller

behavior.footerInfo: Info row starts going horizontal with wrapping. Address columns go side-by-side.
Full horizontal layout. Address section flex-1, right info group w-[640px].
behavior.footerCredits: Three-column layout: copyright left, attribution center, legal right

emptyState.footerInfo: Always visible, logo area shows upload button if no logo set
emptyState.socialIcons: Show Appender when no social items exist
emptyState.navColumns: Show Appender when no columns exist. Each column shows Appender when no items exist.
emptyState.credits: Show Appender when no legal links exist

optionalFields.pattern: shouldDisplay() not used on footer blocks. Conditional rendering used for optional content like subHeading.

editorBehavior.blockNaming: renameBlock() on FooterNavColumn (heading), FooterNavColumnItem (link title), FooterCreditsItem (link title), FooterInfoSocialItem (link title)
editorBehavior.ordering: Footer parent uses TEMPLATE + REQUIRED + useEffect pattern to enforce section order (FooterInfo, FooterNav, FooterCredits)
editorBehavior.fixedSections: Footer parent has no Appender. The three section blocks are fixed and cannot be reordered or added by the user.
editorBehavior.navColumnAppender: FooterNav allows adding/removing columns via Appender. Default template has 6 columns.
editorBehavior.socialAppender: FooterInfoSocial allows adding/removing social items via Appender.

socialMediaItems.srOnly: <span class='sr-only'> with link.title for screen readers
socialMediaItems.newTab: target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer' for external links

footer.name: takt/footer
footer.title: Footer
footer.description: Site footer with logo, contact information, navigation links, social media, and copyright credits

footerInfo.name: takt/footer-info

footerNav.name: takt/footer-nav
footerNav.title: Footer Navigation
footerNav.description: Footer navigation links organized in columns

footerNavColumn.name: takt/footer-nav-column
footerNavColumn.title: Navigation Column
footerNavColumn.description: A column of navigation links with a heading

footerCredits.name: takt/footer-credits
footerCredits.title: Footer Credits

footerCreditsItem.name: takt/footer-credits-item
footerCreditsItem.title: Legal Link
footerCreditsItem.description: Individual legal/policy link in the footer credits bar

footer.notes: All three sections are required and fixed in order. The footer parent renders FooterInfo as a standalone card, then wraps FooterNav and FooterCredits in a shared bottom card div.

footerInfo.notes: FooterInfo uses TEMPLATE pattern for the social block. The social block is a required child that appears in the 'Follow Us' section.

footerNav.notes: FooterNav allows adding/removing columns via Appender. Default starts with 6 columns matching the Figma design.

footerNavColumn.notes: Each column starts with one empty link item. Users can add more via Appender.

footerCredits.notes: Start with 3 empty legal link items (Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Cookies Settings). Users can add/remove via Appender.

footerParentRendering.description: The Footer parent needs special rendering because FooterNav and FooterCredits share a visual bottom card wrapper. The parent must render the bottom card wrapper div around the FooterNav and FooterCredits children.
footerParentRendering.tsxApproach: Do NOT use a single useInnerBlocksProps for all children. Instead, use useSelect to get inner blocks by clientId, then render them in custom layout. The top card (FooterInfo) renders standalone, and the bottom card wrapper div wraps FooterNav + FooterCredits.
footerParentRendering.phpApproach: In PHP, parse $children to separate the three sections. Use parse_blocks() or regex to split, or use a simpler approach: render all children inside the bottom card wrapper and handle FooterInfo separately via its own card styling. ALTERNATIVE: Apply the bottom card wrapper inside Footer.php by splitting children.
footerParentRendering.recommendedApproach: SIMPLEST: Let the Footer parent just render children in a flex column. Apply the bottom card wrapper styling to a div that contains FooterNav and FooterCredits. In TSX, use custom rendering with useSelect to get blocks. In PHP, use a counter or block name check to wrap the second and third children in the bottom card div.
footerParentRendering.phpImplementation: Use render_blocks() to manually render each inner block, wrapping the nav and credits blocks in the bottom card div.

alternativeSimplifiedApproach.description: A simpler alternative: instead of a shared bottom card wrapper, let FooterNav and FooterCredits each apply their own bg-charcoal rounded-3xl styling. Remove the outer 32px rounded wrapper. The visual difference is minimal - the outer rounded-[32px] wrapper adds only a very subtle extra rounding at the outer edge.
alternativeSimplifiedApproach.trade-off: Simpler code vs. exact Figma match. The 32px outer radius wrapping both nav and credits into one card is a subtle visual detail.
alternativeSimplifiedApproach.recommendation: Use the simplified approach for initial implementation. The visual difference between individual rounded cards vs. one wrapped card is minimal and can be refined later.

decisions.question: How to handle the shared bottom card wrapper for FooterNav and FooterCredits?
decisions.decision: Use simplified approach: FooterNav and FooterCredits each have their own bg-charcoal rounded-3xl. No outer 32px wrapper. This avoids complex child block splitting in PHP.
decisions.rationale: The outer 32px rounded container adds minimal visual difference (only the outer edge rounding). The simplified approach is significantly easier to implement in WordPress block editor while maintaining nearly identical visual output. Can be refined later if needed.

decisions.question: Figma shows 25px border radius on cards but Tailwind rounded-3xl is 24px
decisions.rationale: Per token mapping rules: acceptable difference < 2px. rounded-3xl is the closest Tailwind token.

decisions.question: Figma uses Roboto for body text but theme uses General Sans (font-sans)
decisions.decision: Use font-sans (General Sans) as the body font, matching the theme configuration. Do not use Roboto.
decisions.rationale: The theme defines --font-sans as General Sans. All blocks in the codebase use font-sans for body text. Figma may show Roboto as a placeholder but the implementation should follow theme tokens. Per orchestrator guidance: conflictResolution.missingTokens = 'closest'.

decisions.question: Section labels use Anton font at 16px. Theme has text-header-6 (xl/20px) as smallest header size.
decisions.decision: Use font-heading text-base leading-[1.1] for section labels. This gives Anton at 16px/1.1 exactly matching Figma.
decisions.rationale: The 16px size is explicitly shown in Figma. text-base is 16px in Tailwind. We apply font-heading to get Anton, and use arbitrary leading-[1.1] to match the line height.

decisions.question: How to structure address content - as separate fields for each campus or a single rich text block?
decisions.decision: Use a single RichText field (addressContent) that allows bold formatting. Users write campus names in bold and addresses in normal text. The two-column layout for multiple campuses is achieved by the user adding content, but the actual two-column presentation is handled by CSS with a column wrapper.
decisions.rationale: A single RichText is simpler than creating separate address block types. The Figma shows two campuses side-by-side, but this is content-specific. A rich text area with bold support is flexible enough for various address formats. NOTE: If the two-column address layout is critical, this could be revisited to use two separate RichText fields (campus1, campus2).

decisions.question: How to handle the decorative abstract shapes?
decisions.decision: Defer decorative shapes to a visual polish step. They are not part of the core block functionality.
decisions.rationale: The shapes are purely cosmetic, use mix-blend-mode effects, and require exact positioning. They add complexity without content value. Can be added as hardcoded elements in PHP/TSX once core functionality is working.

decisions.question: Footer background gradient uses color-blue (47CBF2) with 50% opacity
decisions.decision: Use inline style or custom CSS class for the gradient: linear-gradient(180deg, transparent 6.73%, rgba(71, 203, 242, 0.5) 100%) with bg-off-white as base
decisions.rationale: The gradient is a specific design element that doesn't map to a simple Tailwind utility. Use bg-off-white on the outer wrapper and apply the gradient via inline style.

decisions.question: Responsive behavior for mobile/tablet not provided in Figma
decisions.decision: Infer standard responsive patterns: stack vertically on mobile, progressively go horizontal on larger screens
decisions.rationale: Per orchestrator guidance: figmaAvailability.tablet = 'infer', figmaAvailability.mobile = 'infer'. Standard responsive convention: maintain same elements, stack on mobile.

decisions.question: Should ImageOrInlineSvg be used for social media items?
decisions.decision: Check if ImageOrInlineSvg exists in @taktdev/components. If not available, use fetchMedia + manual SVG/img rendering. In PHP, use theme_output_svg_or_img().
decisions.rationale: The footer block-type docs reference ImageOrInlineSvg from @taktdev/components. If it does not exist in this project, fall back to the alternative pattern used in this codebase. The developer agent should check availability and use the appropriate approach.

decisions.question: Address section has two campus columns. Should this be two separate RichText fields or one?
decisions.decision: Use two separate RichText fields: addressCampus1 and addressCampus2. This preserves the two-column layout from Figma.
decisions.decision_override: REVISED from DEC-005. After further analysis, two separate fields better match the Figma layout where campuses are distinctly side-by-side.
decisions.rationale: The Figma clearly shows two distinct campus columns with individual bold headings. Two separate RichText fields give precise control over the two-column layout. This is cleaner than trying to parse a single rich text field into columns.

revisedAttributes_footerInfo.note: Based on DEC-010, the footerInfo attributes are revised to use separate campus fields

unresolvedDecisions.question: Is ImageOrInlineSvg available in @taktdev/components in this project?
options: Use fetchMedia + wp_get_attachment_image / manual SVG rendering if not
unresolvedDecisions.defaultIfNotAsked: Developer agent should check availability and use appropriate approach
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Grep search for ImageOrInlineSvg in blocks/ returned no results. The component may exist in @taktdev/components but not yet be used in this project.

unresolvedDecisions.question: Should the decorative abstract shapes be implemented in the initial build?
unresolvedDecisions.reason: Shapes are purely cosmetic, require specific assets and blend modes. Core functionality should work first.
